rulings.Case Title: KATHERINE DAVIS VS. BAXTER HEALTHCARE CORP et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, April 9, 2013, Line 21, DEFENDANT CATHOLIC HEALTHCARE WEST, DBA MERCY MEDICAL CENTER's MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. The motion for summary judgment is denied. Even assuming that there is an MDL exception to the general rule that a federal district court decision is not law of the case on remand and that the July 21, 2011 Order of Judge Carr applies to this case, Defendant has failed to carry its initial burden of showing that Plaintiff cannot prevail on some element of its case because it has failed to present evidence showing that Plaintiff cannot prove her symptoms occurred within one hour after she was given heparin or that Plaintiff cannot prove she could come within the HIT exception to the one hour rule announced in the federal court order. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and is in compliance with Code of Civil Procedure 437c(g) and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/PJB)

rulings.Case Title: DAVID GRASSI VS. JULIEN SIGNES et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion For Order Directing Clerk To Honor Levy And Disburse $10,000.00 Deposit
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, April 9, 2013, Line 23, CLAIMANTs ANTHONY CICCOLINI & JOSEPHINE CICCOLINI's Motion For Order Directing Clerk To Honor Levy And Disburse $10,000.00 Deposit. Third-parties Anthony and Josephine Ciccolini's Motion For Order Directing Clerk To Honor Levy And Disburse $10,000.00 Deposit is denied, Plaintiff's Motion to Vacate Notice of Lien is taken off calendar as moot and Plaintiff's Motion to Vacate Writ of Execution is granted. Because the Ciccolinis did not file a proof of service of renewal notice until March 14, 2013, the writ of execution filed on January 23, 2013 in case #881041 is invalid and of no force and effect. The notice of lien filed in case # 500974 on January 30, 2013 is likewise invalid and of no force and effect but was terminated on March 13, 2013. The renewal of judgment is valid, and these orders are without prejudice to the Ciccolinis right to execute on it. Nothing in the statutes or case law requires contemporaneous service of the renewal notice on the judgment debtor. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/PJB)

rulings.Case Title: ANACIETO LEZAMA ROMAN VS. ANVIL IRON WORKS, INC., A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn To Compel Answers To Post Judgment Discovery And For Award Of Monetary Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, May 7, 2013, at 9:00 am, line 2, PLAINTIFF ANACIETO ROMAN Notice Of Motion To Compel Answers To Post Judgment Discovery And For Award Of Monetary Sanctions Pro Tem Judge Ernst Halperin, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: GRANTED. Defendants Anvil Iron Works, Inc., Jeffrey W. Nieder, and Nida Nieder shall each, separately, within 30 days of notice of entry of this Order: (1) respond to Plaintiff's post judgment interrogatories; and (2) respond to and produce documents requested by Plaintiff's Post-Judgment Demand for Production of Documents and Tangible Things. Plaintiff's request for sanctions against Defendants is GRANTED in the amount of $390.00 Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to ernst@ernsthalperin.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: HBC ASSOCIATES LLC VS. LUCE FORWARD HAMILTON SCRIPPS, LLP et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion To Compel Further Depositions Of Jeffrey Fillerup And William Magston; And Monetary Sanctions Against Mckenna, Long And Aldridge
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, May 7, 2013, at 9:00 am, line 5, Part 1 of 2, Motion To Compel Further Depositions Of Jeffrey Fillerup And William Magston; And Monetary Sanctions Against Mckenna, Long And Aldridge Pro Tem Judge Ernst Halperin, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: GRANTED IN PART AND DENIED IN PART. Deposition of Mr. Fillerup: Mr. Fillerup is a percipient witness in the malpractice litigation brought by HBC against Luce Forward. His verification of Luce Forward's discovery responses allows plaintiff's counsel to question him about the subject matter that he has verified, as well as how and from whom he obtained knowledge of the facts stated in the responses he verified, and what actions he took in order to gather the information stated in the responses and what he did to gather the documents Luce Forward agreed to produce. Nevertheless, his verification of Luce Forward's discovery responses in the malpractice litigation does not waive the attorney-client privilege for communications between him and the third-party witnesses that he represented in the Challas v. Rudolph Matter (managing members William Maston and Jack Damm of ABR) and does not waive the attorney-client privilege for communications between him and his client ABR in the Capital Contribution Dispute. Nor does it waive Mr. Fillerup's work product privilege with respect to his work product on behalf of ABR, Mr. Maston and Mr. Damm in connection with those matters. With these principles in mind: HBC's motion to compel is GRANTED IN PART and DENIED IN PART as follows: Question Nos. 1.1 & 2.1 withdrawn by HBC. GRANTED for Questions Nos. 3.1, 4.1, 5.1 (Question 5.1 asks for the type of information that would have to be included in a privilege log - namely the identity of the ABR representatives with whom Mr. Fillerup communicated. The question does not ask for the substance of any communications with ABR), 8.1, 10.1, 11.1, 15.1, 16.1, 17.1, 18.1, 19.1, 20.1, 21.1, 22.1, and 24.1. DENIED with respect to Questions Nos. 6.1, 7.1, 9.1, 12.1, 13.1, 14.1 (the witness answered the question when rephrased at p. 118:2-8.) and 23.1. Mr. Fillerup may be questioned on topics in addition to the subject matter of the questions for which plaintiff's motion was granted, but all additional questioning shall be limited to no more than 3 hours. Any time spent by Mr. Fillerup's counsel in questioning his client or making objections shall not count towards the three hour limit. --CONTINUED-- =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: HBC ASSOCIATES LLC VS. LUCE FORWARD HAMILTON SCRIPPS, LLP et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion To Compel Further Depositions Of Jeffrey Fillerup And William Magston; And Monetary Sanctions Against Mckenna, Long And Aldridge
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, May 7, 2013, at 9:00 am, line 5, Part 2 of 2, Motion To Compel Further Depositions Of Jeffrey Fillerup And William Magston; And Monetary Sanctions Against Mckenna, Long And Aldridge PART 2 OF 2: -- CONTINUED-- Deposition of Mr. Maston: GRANTED with respect to Questions Nos. 25.1, 26.1, 27.1, 28.1, 29.1, 30.1 (except the witness need not answer the question whether the idea to use the term Hotel Investors Group was Mr. Jelsma's idea and need not answer other questions within 30.1 if they ask whether the idea to use the term Hotel Investor Group was one of ABR's attorneys' idea), 31.1, 32.1, 33.1. DENIED with respect to Questions Nos. 34.1 and 35.1. Additional questioning of Mr.Maston shall be limited to the subject matter of the questions for which plaintiff's motion was granted given that this third-party witness was deposed for a full day already. The additional questioning shall be limited to 1 hour. Any time spent by Mr. Maston's counsel in questioning his client or making objections shall not count towards the one hour limit. Both parties' requests for sanctions are DENIED. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to ernst@ernsthalperin.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. -END- =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: SHAWN CHOY et al VS. CENTURY SURETY COMPANY, AN OHIO CORPORATION et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, April 9, 2013, Line 22, PLAINTIFFs SHAWN CHOY & KAYLA CHA's MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION. The motion for summary adjudication is denied in its entirety. Even assuming the serving of alcohol coverage was triggered given the allegations of the underlying complaint, there is a triable issue of material fact regarding whether the assault and battery exclusion is enforceable in light of the Foreman declaration (the objections to which are overruled). rulings.Case Title: PYN AVIATION, LLC et al VS. ALLIANZ MEXICO, S.A.
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO QUASH SERVICE OF SUMMONS OR STAY OR DISMISS
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, April 9, 2013, Line 17, DEFENDANT PENTA AVIATION SERVICES, INC.'s MOTION TO QUASH SERVICE OF SUMMONS OR STAY OR DISMISS. The motion to quash is granted. Plaintiff's request to conduct discovery directed towards general jurisdiction is denied because plaintiff has not demonstrated any likelihood of producing evidence to establish jurisdiction. Even if defendant did extensive business in Canada with a company based in California (and the evidence does not suggest the companies referred to by plaintiff are based in California), that would not establish general jurisdiction in Canada. The evidence before the Court establishes that defendant lacks any on-going or extensive contacts with California that could support the exercise of jurisdiction, and the subject matter of the lawsuit has no connection to any activity by defendant in California. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/PJB)

rulings.Case Title: WHOLE FOODS MARKET GROUP, INC. VS. WEB2WEB MARKETING,INC. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Enforce Arb Provision;
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, April 9, 2013, Line 26, DEFENDANT WEB2WEB MARKETING,INC.'s Motion To Enforce Arbitration Provision. The motion is granted and the case stayed as to account number 40940, which was never governed by the Short Term Agreement. As to that account, defendant has shown an agreement to arbitrate. Even assuming that plaintiff has shown the agreement was procedurally unconscionable, it has not show the required substantive unconscionability. Nor has defendant shown that the employee who entered into the agreement on plaintiff's behalf lacked authority to do so. The motion is denied with respect to the remaining accounts because the Short Term Agreement, which did not include an agreement to arbitrate, superseded the TSA, and defendant has not demonstrated that the parties agreed to have the TSA's arbitration provision spring back into force on the termination of the Short Term Agreement. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/PJB)
